title: Create an automation to create a new item across multiple boards and to specific group

posts.author: alyssalovette (Alyssa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I work for a video production company. Each video has it’s unique group on one board. We also have a GFX board to submit GFX requests for specific videos

I’d like an automation that creates a mirrored item to a specific group on another board when a new GFX request is submitted from the GFX board. Is that possible?

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @alyssalovette

Yes, this is possible. You can use a cross board automation to create an item on your video board whenever a new GFX request is submitted. That item can then be linked back to the GFX request using a Connect Boards column, and any needed fields can be shown with Mirror columns.

You can also scope the automation to a specific group if needed.

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Monday Wizard

alyssalovette:

Hello,

I work for a video production company. Each video has it’s unique group on one board. We also have a GFX board to submit GFX requests for specific videos

I’d like an automation that creates a mirrored item to a specific group on another board when a new GFX request is submitted from the GFX board. Is that possible?

posts.author: alyssalovette (Alyssa)
posts.cookedHtml:
drtanvisachar:

You can use a cross board automation to create an item on your video board whenever a new GFX request is submitted. That item can then be linked back to the GFX request using a Connect Boards column, and any needed fields can be shown with Mirror columns.

Thank you for your response!

I’m having trouble with understanding ’scoping the automation to a specific group’. How does that work?

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
@alyssalovette You’re very close
Native automations can create the item on the target board and you can choose a specific group but they cannot dynamically route to different groups based on the source item’s group.

The usual workaround is
Create the item on the target board into a holding group
Use a Connect Boards column to link it back to the original item
Optionally use a Status or Dropdown on the source board to indicate the destination group
Then use a second automation to move the item to the correct group based on that value

If you need the group mapping to stay fully dynamic at scale, this is where a Make or API based flow works best.

If you want, you can also book a short session with our team and we can walk through the setup and recommend the cleanest approach for your boards. you can shedule here Calendly
